ops.text: Workflow schema-bound wrapper subagents get force-concluded mid-wait: a sonnet wrapper babysitting a long codex exec run (via Bash run_in_background + repeated TaskOutput polls) was forced by a StructuredOutput enforcement reminder to emit its final answer ~3min in, and its reaping killed the child codex process mid-task (pid gone, no last.txt, work lost — luckily still in read-only preflight). The codex plugin's real codex-wrapper isn't loaded in this session, so hand-rolled wrappers are the only sol lane. Structural workaround now in use: detach codex (setsid/nohup or main-session run_in_background) so it survives wrapper death, and have the wrapper wait via ONE background until-loop notification instead of many poll turns. A workflow-native long-wait primitive (or a turn-budget exemption while a declared background child runs) would remove the trap.
